steps.title: Place the examples in context
steps.content: Serrano argues that walking decisions occur in complex contexts and that current understanding is not robust enough. The references to demographic characteristics and sidewalk width appear as examples of factors that individual studies have emphasized.

steps.title: Read and so on carefully
steps.content: The phrase "and so on" shows that demographic characteristics and sidewalk width are not the whole explanation. They are two possible influences among many conditions and needs that can affect whether people decide to walk. The passage's point is that studying one factor at a time may miss the broader context of walking decisions.

steps.title: Avoid overstating their role
steps.content: Choice A wrongly says Serrano calls the factors overemphasized, even though the passage says the overall understanding is incomplete. Choice C adds an unsupported claim about what people rarely consider, and Choice D makes the factors unique to Rome. The remaining choice describes them as important but partial influences. The answer is B.
